payload.answer: This appears to be a duplicate rather than a true conflict: both memories say that Sam from Goldman likes Bill Evans. Safe options are: link_duplicate to connect the new wording to the existing memory as the same fact, keep_existing to retain only the existing memory, add_anyway to store the new memory separately despite the duplication, edit to revise the new memory before saving, or cancel to take no action.
citations: Existing memory: Sam from Goldman mentioned that he likes Bill Evans.
New: Sam from Goldman said he likes Bill Evans.

entity_resolution.reason: The existing memory and new memory express the same durable fact with only wording differences.

details: Identified the new memory as semantically equivalent to the existing memory.
No overwrite or auto-supersession was applied.
